---
title: "prexorctl role update"
description: "Replace the permission list on an existing custom role"
---

<!-- auto-generated by tools/gen-cli-docs.ts — do not edit by hand. -->

Replace the permission list on an existing custom role.
Built-in roles (ADMIN, OPERATOR, VIEWER) cannot be modified — the controller
rejects the request with a 422. Use `role create` to derive a custom
role from a built-in one.
Example:
  prexorctl role update viewer-readonly \
    --permissions groups.view,instances.view,nodes.view,crashes.view

## Usage

```text
prexorctl role update <name> [flags]
```

## Flags

```text
-h, --help                 help for update
        --permissions string   Comma-separated permission list (replaces the existing set)
```

## Global flags

```text
--ascii               Use ASCII glyphs only (no unicode box drawing, sparklines, etc.)
        --context string      Override active context for this invocation
    -c, --controller string   Override controller URL
    -j, --json                Output in JSON format
        --no-color            Disable colored output
    -t, --token string        Override auth token
    -v, --verbose             Show HTTP request/response details
```
